description: Group Workout Detail Page - Show participant workout details (type, start time, duration) when selecting a group workout from the log

userStories.title: Create API endpoint for group workout detail
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a new GET /group-workouts/:id endpoint that returns a group workout along with each participant's individual workout details so the frontend can display them.
acceptanceCriteria: New GET /group-workouts/:id endpoint exists in services/api/src/routes/group-workouts.ts and requires authentication via requireAuth middleware
Response includes the GroupWorkout fields plus a 'participants' array of enriched workout details
Each participant entry includes: uid, displayName, workoutType, startedAt, endedAt, and status
Endpoint fetches individual Workout documents by IDs from GroupWorkout.workoutIds and UserProfile documents by UIDs from GroupWorkout.memberUids using getDb() from lib/firestore.ts
Returns 404 JSON response if the group workout document does not exist in Firestore
Returns 403 JSON response if the authenticated user's uid is not in the group workout's memberUids array
Unit tests cover success, 404, and 403 cases following the existing vi.mock + vi.hoisted + buildApp() + supertest pattern
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Make Group Workout Log rows clickable on Burn Buddy page
userStories.description: As a user viewing my Burn Buddy detail page, I want to click a group workout entry so I can navigate to the full details of that session.
acceptanceCriteria: Each row in the Group Workout Log section on /burn-buddies/[id] page is wrapped in a Next.js Link component
Clicking a row navigates to /burn-buddies/[id]/group-workouts/[gwId] where gwId is the group workout's id
Row has a hover state indicating it's interactive (e.g., background highlight to bg-gray-800 and/or a right chevron icon)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Make Group Workout Log rows clickable on Burn Squad page
userStories.description: As a user viewing my Burn Squad detail page, I want to click a group workout entry so I can navigate to the full details of that session.
acceptanceCriteria: Each row in the Group Workout Log section on /burn-squads/[id] page is wrapped in a Next.js Link component
Clicking a row navigates to /burn-squads/[id]/group-workouts/[gwId] where gwId is the group workout's id
Row has a hover state matching the Burn Buddy page treatment from US-002 (same styling pattern)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build group workout detail page for Burn Buddies
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a detail page for a group workout that shows what each buddy did during the session.
acceptanceCriteria: New Next.js page exists at apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-buddies/[id]/group-workouts/[gwId]/page.tsx
Page header shows the formatted date of the group workout and a back link that navigates to /burn-buddies/[id]
Page fetches data from GET /group-workouts/:gwId API endpoint using the existing API helper in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts

Displays a card for each participant showing: display name (linked to /burn-buddies/[id]), workout type (e.g. Running, HIIT), start time formatted as human-readable time (e.g. 7:32 AM), and duration (e.g. 45 min) computed from endedAt - startedAt
Workouts with status 'active' and no endedAt show an 'In Progress' badge with green accent instead of duration
Shows a loading skeleton or spinner while fetching data
Shows an error message if the group workout is not found (404) or user is not authorized (403)
Matches the existing dark theme (dark backgrounds, white/gray text) used on Burn Buddy detail pages
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build group workout detail page for Burn Squads
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a detail page for a squad group workout that shows what each squad member did during the session.
acceptanceCriteria: New Next.js page exists at apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-squads/[id]/group-workouts/[gwId]/page.tsx
Page header shows the formatted date of the group workout and a back link that navigates to /burn-squads/[id]
Page fetches data from GET /group-workouts/:gwId API endpoint using the existing API helper in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts
Displays a card for each participant showing: display name (linked to /burn-squads/[id]), workout type, start time, and duration — same fields as the buddy detail page
Workouts with status 'active' and no endedAt show an 'In Progress' badge with green accent instead of duration
Handles squads with 3+ members gracefully with cards stacking vertically and no horizontal overflow
Shows loading and error states matching the buddy page pattern from US-004
Matches the existing dark theme used on Burn Squad detail pages
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
